title: Wireshark Filters
description: Display and capture filters for isolating specific traffic in Wireshark.
fullExplanation: Wireshark has two filter types: Capture filters (BPF syntax, applied during capture — reduces file size) and Display filters (Wireshark syntax, applied after capture — doesn't discard packets). Display filters are more powerful and flexible. They support protocol fields, comparison operators, logical operators, and functions. Mastering filters is essential for efficient packet analysis.

displayFilters.description: Traffic to or from specific IP

captureFilters.description: Exclude SSH (avoid capturing your own session)

keyFacts: Display filters: use after capture, don't discard packets, very powerful
Capture filters: BPF syntax, applied during capture, reduces file size
Follow Stream: right-click packet → Follow → TCP/UDP/TLS Stream — see full conversation
Colorize: View → Coloring Rules — customize colors for different traffic types
Export Objects: File → Export Objects → HTTP — extract files transferred over HTTP
Statistics → Protocol Hierarchy: see breakdown of all protocols in capture
